pattern: A monotonically decreasing stack of candidate left endpoints lets a right-to-left sweep pop the farthest reachable start for each position, finding the widest valid span in linear time.
hints: A good left endpoint is one no later position can do better than; what makes an index dominated by an earlier one?
Only indices forming a strictly decreasing prefix of values can ever be the optimal left endpoint.
Push indices whose values strictly decrease onto a stack, then sweep from the right popping every stack index whose value is at most the current one, tracking the largest gap.

edge_inputs.description: ramp spans most of the array
equal endpoints give a wide ramp
two equal elements form a width-one ramp
two strictly decreasing elements yield zero
best ramp uses a small early value, not the global minimum
all equal values span the whole array

stress.description: random values over a moderate range to create many overlapping ramps
stress.note: exposes O(n^2) pair scans under the time limit
